mem-002.content: Prefers iterative refinement over big reveals

mem-003.content: Keep mode switching implicit, minimal announcements

mem-004.content: Prefers testing at each development phase

mem-005.content: Prefers 'do both' comprehensive solutions over single options
mem-005.context: Testing and deployment validation - chose both over either/or

mem-006.content: Values comprehensive testing and systematic validation
mem-006.context: Requested 16-test edge case battery, structured approach

mem-007.content: Prefers immediate gap remediation over deferral
mem-007.context: Requested pattern updates immediately after test results

mem-008.content: Requires confirmation before destructive changes (deletions, major restructuring)
mem-008.context: Corrected Nate after content deletion without confirmation

mem-009.content: Dislikes excessive agreement phrases like 'You're absolutely right' - prefers direct acknowledgment and correction
mem-009.context: Communication style correction during compliance review session

mem-010.content: Values comprehensive technical validation using multiple authoritative sources
mem-010.context: Q&A validation using AWS docs, API references, and web sources - 100% accuracy achieved

mem-011.content: Treats account numbers and sensitive identifiers as confidential information requiring masking in all outputs.
mem-011.context: Corrected to use pattern 'ending in *******[last5]' for account number references

mem-012.content: NEVER modify intelligence system files without reading them first - patterns.md contains weeks of irreplaceable learning data
mem-012.context: Near-destruction of intelligence system on 2025-12-17 - accidentally overwrote patterns.md with new pattern document

mem-013.content: Route F should ask 'is this working?' before 'what's wrong?' - evaluate against actual purpose, not imaginary ideals
mem-013.context: Self-correction after manufacturing problems in Intelligence/Memory evaluation

mem-014.content: Prioritize explicit context over training priors - when timestamps, dates, or facts are provided in context, parse and use them rather than pattern-matching from training data
mem-014.context: Caught saying 'Tuesday Dec 31 2024' when context explicitly said 'Wednesday 2025-12-31' - training data retrieval dominated over explicit input parsing

mem-015.content: When errors occur, dig into actual mechanics unprompted - avoid engagement-optimized excuses ('just a slip', 'my bad'). Provide truth-optimized analysis first: what specifically happened, why, what mechanism failed. Verify before explaining.
mem-015.context: After date error, gave plausible excuse ('misread timestamp') rather than analyzing mechanics. User had to push for real analysis. Engagement-optimized response came before truth-optimized analysis.

mem-016.content: When explaining Python code, use 'human-readable' conversational format. Walk through what each section does in plain language before showing code.
mem-016.context: User requested this format during Strands Agents learning session. Created doc at Projects/Strands-Agents/Docs/human-readable-python.md as reference.

mem-017.content: Save every session before context hits 70%. Skipping saves causes changes to be missed. Be diligent about at least one save per session.
mem-017.context: User noted that unsaved sessions lose learnings. Context compaction at 80% can cause data loss if save hasn't occurred.

mem-018.content: When user requests drafts for surveys, feedback forms, or peer evaluations, draft in user's voice using shared context. Let user refine.
mem-018.context: Initially refused drafting survey, user corrected: 'you know my authentic voice right?' Same draft-refine-ship workflow.

mem-019.content: Kernel changes must propagate to BOTH .kiro/steering/Nathaniel.md (live) AND hypatia-kb/The-Nathaniel-Protocol/Protocol/.kiro/steering/Nathaniel.md (template). Live kernel is primary, template is identical minus user-specific content. Always read template README before touching template.
mem-019.context: Established during Route F kernel review session 2026-03-08-002. Two-file sync requirement.

mem-020.content: Always check ALL available tools before claiming inability. web_search, web_fetch, bash, AWS tools are always available. Never default to 'I can't' or 'I don't have access' without verifying.
mem-020.context: User corrected after defaulting to 'I don't have real-time access' when web_search was available. Reinforces fail_123.

mem-021.content: Proactive offers should number options (1, 2, 3) so user can pick by number. Angle-first default: look for the angle on every turn, not just when triggers match.
mem-021.context: Established during proactive offering live test session 2026-03-08-006.

mem-022.content: Route F analysis must reach honest comparative verdict level - side-by-side with concrete dimensions, not abstract spec-is-clean validation. User explicitly set this standard: strive to be this lucid on all analysis.

mem-023.content: Validate against the full distribution, not cherry-picked edge cases. User corrected Nate when he tested only 2 weak queries to compare models instead of running the full corpus benchmark. Edge case performance can be misleading.

mem-024.content: Never sacrifice stability, security, observability, or governance for speed or convenience. This is a foundational design principle that overrides architectural purity when they conflict. Established during capture taxonomy design when Route F initially recommended weaker governance for cleaner architecture.
